prose:
Frontier-orbital calculation on benzene (12 atoms: 6 C + 6 H) using DFT, tier 'standard' (B3LYP/def2-TZVP), exact four-center integrals (no density fitting), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1, in PySCF via chemkit. Geometry: user-supplied .xyz, used as-is (single point, not re-optimized at DFT level). SCF converged in 8 cycles. No warnings reported. Integrity status: ok, trustworthy: true.

Headline result — HOMO–LUMO gap: 6.6310857 eV (Koopmans-style orbital energy gap).

HOMO (index 20): −7.0468329 eV
LUMO (index 21): −0.41574728 eV
HOMO–LUMO gap: 6.6310857 eV

The HOMO and HOMO−1 are nearly degenerate (−7.0468 vs −7.0473 eV), consistent with the doubly degenerate e₁g π-HOMO of benzene (D₆h). The LUMO and LUMO+1 are likewise nearly degenerate (−0.4157 vs −0.4151 eV), corresponding to the e₂u π* LUMOs.

Output JSON: benzene_frontier_dft_b3lyp_def2tzvp.json
CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/benzene/benzene.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1
